project.name: Musa.Core
project.description: Musa.Core is a kernel-mode Windows API thunk library that reimplements Kernel32, Advapi32, and other Win32 APIs on top of ntoskrnl. It provides Zw Routines, Rtl Series, KernelBase, and Kernel32 thunks (Phases 1-6) with NuGet integration, debug logging, and builds for Debug/Release on x86, x64, and ARM64. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: universal.h
nodes.summary: Global precompiled header for Musa.Core static library, defining MUSA macros (MUSA_NAME, IAT symbols, logging), global constants (MUSA_TAG), and including core headers (Veil, Musa.CoreLite, Musa.Core.h).

nodes.name: Musa.Core.TestForDriver.cpp
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test driver for Musa.Core kernel-mode API thunks, exercising heap management, synchronization primitives (critical sections, SRW locks, events, mutexes, semaphores), processes/threads, file I/O, path/string conversions, environment variables, console/NLS, time functions, and pointer encoding.
nodes.languageNotes: Single 1183-line RunTests function tests dozens of Win32 API thunks via KTEST_EXPECT validation macros.

nodes.name: RunTests
nodes.summary: Main test routine executing hundreds of API thunk validations across heap, sync, process/thread, file I/O, NLS, time, console, and environment variable operations.

nodes.name: DriverUnload
nodes.summary: Driver unload handler that calls MusaCoreShutdown and frees allocated test resources.

nodes.name: BootDriverReinitialize
nodes.summary: Boot-time reinitialization callback that invokes MusaCoreStartup and runs tests on reinitialization pass.

nodes.name: DriverEntry
nodes.summary: Test driver entry point that calls MusaCoreStartup and optionally runs tests immediately or registers for boot reinitialization.

nodes.name: Musa.Core.cpp
nodes.summary: Musa.Core library entry point implementing MusaCoreStartup and MusaCoreShutdown, which orchestrate driver lifecycle: environment block setup/teardown, thread notification, and lightweight core initialization.

nodes.name: MusaCoreStartup
nodes.summary: Core library startup sequence: calls MusaCoreLiteStartup then EnvironmentBlockSetup, with rollback on failure.

nodes.name: MusaCoreShutdown
nodes.summary: Core library shutdown handler: calls EnvironmentBlockTeardown then MusaCoreLiteShutdown.

nodes.name: Musa.Core.h
nodes.summary: Public header declaring the MusaCoreStartup and MusaCoreShutdown API entry points with architecture guards.

nodes.name: Musa.Core.Nothing.cpp
nodes.summary: Empty source file (0 non-empty lines), used as a build placeholder for the Musa.Core static library.

nodes.name: Musa.Core.SystemEnvironmentBlock.cpp
nodes.summary: Environment block setup/teardown at the driver level: initializes Fiber Local Storage (FLS), process/thread environment blocks, shared heap, thread notification callback, and a kernel callback object for thread notifications.

nodes.name: ThreadNotifyCallback
nodes.summary: Thread creation/deletion notification callback that forwards events via ExNotifyCallback.

nodes.name: EnvironmentBlockSetup
nodes.summary: Sets up the full Musa environment: initializes driver runtime, callback object, process environment block, shared heap, and thread creation notification. Tears down on failure.

nodes.name: EnvironmentBlockTeardown
nodes.summary: Tears down the Musa environment: removes thread notification routine, destroys heap, dereferences callback object, and cleans up process environment blocks.

nodes.name: Musa.Core.SystemEnvironmentBlock.Fls.cpp
nodes.summary: Fiber Local Storage (FLS) management implementation: FLS slot allocation with bitmap tracking, cleanup of stale FLS data, and per-slot callback invocation on thread/process teardown.

nodes.name: FlsCreate
nodes.summary: Initializes FLS infrastructure: creates an empty list head and a bitmap for slot tracking.

nodes.name: FlsCleanup
nodes.summary: Cleans up FLS data: acquires exclusive lock, removes all entries from the FLS list, invokes per-slot callbacks via RtlProcessFlsData, and frees heap-allocated callback pointer arrays.

nodes.name: FlsDataCleanup
nodes.summary: Performs per-FLS-data cleanup: atomically transfers slot callbacks, invokes them safely, removes the entry from the list, and frees memory.

nodes.name: Musa.Core.SystemEnvironmentBlock.Fls.h
nodes.summary: Fiber Local Storage (FLS) header declaring the PRTL_FLS_DATA and PFLS_CALLBACK_FUNCTION structures used for per-thread FLS slot tracking.

nodes.name: Musa.Core.SystemEnvironmentBlock.h
nodes.summary: Public header declaring EnvironmentBlockSetup and EnvironmentBlockTeardown API functions for driver-level environment block lifecycle management.

nodes.name: Musa.Core.SystemEnvironmentBlock.Process.cpp
nodes.summary: Process Environment Block (PEB/KPEB) management: allocates and initializes per-process state including environment variables list, module paths, resource lock, rundown protection, system heap, and PEB lock primitives (exclusive/shared).

nodes.name: ProcessEnvironmentBlockSetup
nodes.summary: Allocates and initializes the KPEB structure: configures image path, command line, environment variable linked list, resource lock, rundown protection, system heap, and thread environment block. Rolls back on failure.

nodes.name: ProcessEnvironmentBlockTeardown
nodes.summary: Tears down the KPEB: waits for rundown protection, closes handles, cleans up FLS, frees environment variables and module path strings, destroys heap, and releases the resource lock.

nodes.name: RtlGetCurrentPeb
nodes.summary: Returns the current thread's KPEB pointer via inline wrapper.

nodes.name: RtlAcquirePebLockExclusive
nodes.summary: Acquires the PEB exclusive lock by entering critical region and acquiring the KPEB resource in exclusive mode.

nodes.name: RtlReleasePebLockExclusive
nodes.summary: Releases the PEB exclusive lock and leaves the critical region.

nodes.name: RtlAcquirePebLockShared
nodes.summary: Acquires the PEB shared lock by entering critical region and acquiring the KPEB resource in shared mode.

nodes.name: RtlReleasePebLockShared
nodes.summary: Releases the PEB shared lock and leaves the critical region.

nodes.name: Musa.Core.SystemEnvironmentBlock.Process.h
nodes.summary: Process Environment Block header defining the KPEB structure, ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLE_ENTRY linked list node, and declarations for PEB lock and environment variable management APIs.

nodes.name: ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLE_ENTRY
nodes.summary: Linked list entry structure for environment variable storage, containing Link (LIST_ENTRY), Name (UNICODE_STRING), and Value (UNICODE_STRING).

nodes.name: Musa.Core.SystemEnvironmentBlock.Thread.cpp
nodes.summary: Thread Environment Block (TEB/KTEB) management: lookaside list allocation, AVL table-based thread tracking, TEB retrieval by thread/process ID, thread creation/deletion notification handling with FLS cleanup, and TEB spin lock primitives.

nodes.name: Compare
nodes.summary: Comparison callback for the AVL generic table used to look up KTEB entries by thread ID.

nodes.name: Allocate
nodes.summary: Memory allocation callback for the KTEB AVL table, using a lookaside list for efficient KTEB allocation.

nodes.name: Free
nodes.summary: Memory deallocation callback for the KTEB AVL table, freeing KTEB entries back to the lookaside list.

nodes.name: ThreadNotifyCallbackForTeb
nodes.summary: Thread creation/deletion notification callback that inserts/removes KTEB entries from the AVL table and processes FLS data cleanup on thread exit.

nodes.name: ThreadEnvironmentBlockSetup
nodes.summary: Initializes TEB subsystem: creates lookaside list for KTEB allocation, initializes AVL generic table, and registers callback notification for thread creation.

nodes.name: ThreadEnvironmentBlockTeardown
nodes.summary: Tears down TEB subsystem: unregisters callback, deletes all AVL table entries, and deletes the lookaside list.

nodes.name: RtlGetCurrentTeb
nodes.summary: Retrieves the current thread's KTEB by looking up (thread ID, process ID) in the AVL table. Creates a new entry on first access, emitting ExNotifyCallback via the thread callback.

nodes.name: RtlAcquireTebLockExclusive
nodes.summary: Acquires the TEB spin lock in exclusive mode.

nodes.name: RtlReleaseTebLockExclusive
nodes.summary: Releases the TEB exclusive spin lock, restoring the previous IRQL.

nodes.name: RtlAcquireTebLockShared
nodes.summary: Acquires the TEB spin lock in shared mode.

nodes.name: RtlReleaseTebLockShared
nodes.summary: Releases the TEB shared spin lock, restoring the previous IRQL.

nodes.name: Musa.Core.SystemEnvironmentBlock.Thread.h
nodes.summary: Thread Environment Block header declaring the KTEB structure and API declarations for TEB setup/teardown, RtlGetCurrentTeb, and TEB spin lock primitives (exclusive/shared acquire/release).

nodes.name: Fnv1aHash
nodes.summary: Compile-time FNV-1a hash function template for null-terminated strings, supporting both regular and _countof-bounded hashing.

nodes.name: Musa.Utilities.Overlay.cpp

nodes.name: RunTaskOnLowIrql
nodes.summary: Queues a work item to run a task function at PASSIVE_LEVEL, supporting both I/O work items and legacy EX work items, and waits synchronously for completion.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what Musa.Core is and why it exists. This document explains that Musa.Core is a kernel-mode Windows API thunk library that reimplements Kernel32, Advapi32, and other Win32 APIs on top of ntoskrnl. The architecture diagram shows the flow from a Kernel Driver through MusaCoreStartup into the Musa.Core StaticLibrary, then through Musa.CoreLite and Musa.Veil down to ntoskrnl.exe. This top-level orientation provides the mental model needed before diving into any code.

tour.title: System Architecture Detail
tour.description: The system architecture document deepens the README's overview with Mermaid component diagrams and formal layer descriptions. It documents the layered architecture spanning Consumer drivers, the Musa.Core library, its dependency on Musa.CoreLite and Musa.Veil, and the underlying ntoskrnl.exe kernel. This document is the most-referenced non-code artifact in the project (fan-in 7), which reflects its role as the definitive reference for how components relate to each other.

tour.title: Library Entry Point
tour.description: Musa.Core.cpp implements MusaCoreStartup and MusaCoreShutdown, the two public API entry points every kernel-mode consumer driver calls to initialize and tear down the thunk library. MusaCoreStartup orchestrates environment block setup, thread notification registration, and lightweight core initialization, while MusaCoreShutdown reverses each step. The companion header file Musa.Core.h declares these two functions with architecture guards to ensure correct compilation for x64 and ARM64 targets.

tour.title: Core Framework: Environment Orchestration
tour.description: Building on the entry-point flow, the SystemEnvironmentBlock module orchestrates the three critical subsystems needed before any Win32 thunks can function: Fiber Local Storage (FLS) for per-fiber state, Process Environment Block (PEB/KPEB) for per-process state, and Thread Environment Block (TEB/KTEB) for per-thread state. The implementation file calls through to sub-modules while the header declares the top-level EnvironmentBlockSetup and EnvironmentBlockTeardown functions that MusaCoreStartup invokes. This is the bridge between the startup entry point and the actual kernel environment initialization.

tour.title: Process, Thread, and Fiber State Management
tour.description: The three concrete environment subsystems that EnvironmentBlockSetup activates. The Process module allocates per-process PEB structures with environment variable lists, module paths, resource locks, and heap primitives. The Thread module manages per-thread TEB structures using a custom AVL tree for efficient lookups by thread ID, and registers thread creation/deletion callbacks. The FLS module implements Fiber Local Storage slot allocation using bitmap tracking and stale data cleanup. Together these modules build the user-mode-like execution context that Win32 thunks depend on, all running inside the kernel address space.

tour.title: Shared Utilities Layer
tour.description: The utility layer provides reusable primitives used across the entire library. Musa.Utilities.h defines pointer encoding and decoding helpers (FastEncodePointer, FastDecodePointer) using rotate-based obfuscation along with the Fnv1aHash function. Musa.Utilities.Overlay.cpp implements RunTaskOnLowIrql, which executes callbacks at PASSIVE_LEVEL by queueing work items through the I/O manager — essential for any operation that cannot run at elevated IRQL. Musa.Utilities.PEParser.h provides PE image parsing helpers, notably ImageEnumerateExports for iterating DLL export tables, which is used during dynamic thunk resolution.

tour.title: Thunk Architecture and Internal Headers
tour.description: This step reveals the thunk implementation pattern that every API reimplementation follows. KernelBase.Private.h is the single most-depended-upon file in the project (fan-in 24), defining the BaseSetLastNTError macro that maps NTSTATUS results to Win32 error codes — the fundamental error plumbing for every thunk. The universal.h precompiled header (in the StaticLibraryForDriver directory) defines the MUSA_NAME macro used to declare thunk export names, the IAT hook table symbols, and debug logging infrastructure. The internal headers under Thunks/Internal/ declare the function signatures for each API family (KernelBase.System.h, KernelBase.Thread.h, KernelBase.File.h, etc.), establishing the contract that the implementation files fulfill.

tour.title: KernelBase: File I/O and Handle Management
tour.description: The file I/O thunk file is one of the largest in the project, implementing CreateFileW, ReadFile, WriteFile, GetFileType, FindFirstFileExW, SetFilePointerEx, and over 20 more Win32 file APIs by translating each call into equivalent Zw/Xxx system-call sequences through Musa.Veil. The handle management thunks provide GetStdHandle, SetStdHandle, CloseHandle, DuplicateHandle, and CompareObjectHandles — the fundamental handle lifecycle operations. Together these demonstrate the core pattern: wrap ntoskrnl primitives behind Win32 API signatures, handling parameter validation, error translation, and kernel-mode-specific concerns like handle table access at the right IRQL.

tour.title: KernelBase: Synchronization, System Info, and Thread Management
tour.description: The synchronization thunk hub implements Sleep/SleepEx, WaitForSingle/MultipleObjects, one-time initialization, SRW locks, critical sections, and condition variables using kernel-mode primitives like KeWaitForMultipleObjects and ERESOURCE. The system information thunks provide GetNativeSystemInfo, GetSystemInfo, VerifyVersionInfoW, and command-line/environment variable access. The thread management thunks deliver OpenThread, Set/GetThreadPriority, TerminateThread, SuspendThread, ResumeThread, GetThreadTimes, and GetThreadContext — covering the full thread lifecycle. These three files form the core of the KernelBase API Thunks layer (fan-out 4 each) and handle the most commonly used Win32 synchronization and system query APIs.

tour.title: Ntdll Internal Declarations
tour.description: The Ntdll internal headers define the data structures and function declarations for the lowest-level thunks. Ntdll.Heap.h (fan-in 4) defines the HEAP and HEAP_ENTRY data structures and declares RtlCreateHeap, RtlAllocateHeap, and all companion heap functions for kernel mode. Ntdll.Path.h declares path conversion thunks including RtlDosPathNameToNtPathName_U and RtlGetFullPathName_U that bridge Win32 DOS paths to NT device paths. Ntdll.Synchronize.h declares the synchronization primitive thunks (SRW lock, critical section, condition variable) for kernel-mode use. These headers are the internal contract that the corresponding .cpp implementation files fulfill, and they are the most widely-referenced Ntdll headers after the KernelBase ones.

tour.title: Ntdll: Heap, Path, and Error Handling
tour.description: The three foundational Ntdll implementation files. Ntdll.Heap.cpp implements the comprehensive RtlAllocateHeap, RtlFreeHeap, RtlReAllocateHeap, and RtlSizeHeap thunks — every memory allocation in the entire library flows through these, backed by ExAllocatePool2 and ExFreePool. Ntdll.Path.cpp provides path manipulation including RtlGetFullPathName_U, RtlGetLongPathName_U, and RtlGetNtPathFromDosPath, bridging the Win32 namespace to the NT namespace used by ntoskrnl. Ntdll.Error.cpp implements the error translation layer: RtlGetLastWin32Error, RtlSetLastNtStatus, and vectored exception handler registration — the error plumbing that every thunk relies on to translate kernel NTSTATUS codes into Win32 error codes.

tour.title: Ntdll: Library Loading and Synchronization
tour.description: The library loader thunk implements DLL resolution in kernel mode: LdrUnloadDll, LdrGetDllHandle, LdrGetProcedureAddress, and related module resolution routines that bridge LoadLibrary/GetProcAddress semantics to ntoskrnl's image loading primitives. The synchronization implementation files provide kernel-mode critical sections using ERESOURCE with heap allocation (RtlInitializeCriticalSection, RtlEnterCriticalSection, RtlLeaveCriticalSection) and slim reader/writer locks using EX_PUSH_LOCK (RtlAcquireSRWLockExclusive, RtlAcquireSRWLockShared, etc.). Together these files complete the Ntdll thunks layer by providing the library and synchronization APIs that KernelBase thunks layer on top of.

tour.title: Kernel-Mode Test Driver
tour.description: The test driver is a full WDM kernel-mode driver that validates the thunk library end-to-end. Musa.Core.TestForDriver.cpp implements DriverEntry, DriverUnload, BootDriverReinitialize, and the comprehensive RunTests function that exercises heap management, synchronization primitives, thread operations, file I/O, and system information APIs — all running at IRQL PASSIVE_LEVEL inside a KMDF driver framework. The companion INF file configures the test driver for installation as a legacy NT driver. This is how every thunk implementation gets validated before shipping: by running it inside an actual kernel-mode driver and verifying the results against expected Win32 behavior.

tour.title: Build System, CI/CD, and Distribution
tour.description: The project uses MSBuild with the Mile.Project SDK for compilation. The static library vcxproj compiles all Musa.Core thunks into a single static library (.lib) and publishes the headers, PDBs, and library artifacts. The GitHub Actions CI pipeline runs on a windows-2025 runner triggered by pushes to main and version tags, executing checkout, build configuration, project compilation, and test deployment steps. The NuGet package manifest (Musa.Core.nuspec) packages the static library for consumption, depending on Musa.CoreLite for the runtime bridge components. Together these build, test, and distribute the library for other kernel-mode driver projects to consume as a NuGet dependency.
